articles.title: After the fire
articles.question: What was the danger to the villages after the fire?火灾后村庄面临什么危险？

sentences.text: Firemen had been fighting the forest fire for nearly three weeks before they could get it under control.
A short time before, great trees had covered the countryside for miles around.
Now, smoke still rose up from the warm ground over the desolate hills.
Winter was coming on and the hills threatened the surrounding villages with destruction, for heavy rain would not only wash away the soil but would cause serious floods as well.
When the fire had at last been put out, the forest authorities ordered several tons of a special type of grass seed which would grow quickly.
The seed was sprayed over the ground in huge quantities by aeroplanes.
The planes had been planting seed for nearly a month when it began to rain.
By then, however, in many places the grass had already taken root.
In place of the great trees which had been growing there for centuries, patches of green had begun to appear in the blackened soil.

articles.original_chinese: 消防队员们同那场森林大火搏斗了将近3个星期才最后把火势控制住。就在不久之前，参天大树还覆盖着方圆数英里的土地。而现在，发热的地面上仍然升腾着烟雾，弥漫在荒凉的山丘上。冬季即将来临，这些山丘对周围的村庄具有毁灭性的威胁，因为大雨不仅会冲走土壤，而且还会引起严重的水灾。在大火最后被扑灭后，森林管理当局订购了好几吨一种生长迅速的特殊类型的草籽。飞机把这种草籽大量地撒播在地上。飞机撒播近一个月后，开始下起雨来。然而到那时，很多地方的草已经生了根。一片片的绿草开始出现在这片烧焦的土地上，代替了多少世纪以来一直生长在那里的参天大树。地址： 38.190.226.110

articles.title: She was not amused
articles.question: Why did Jenny want to leave the wedding reception?为什么珍妮想离开婚礼招待会？

sentences.text: Jeremy Hampden has a large circle of friends and is very popular at parties.
Everybody admires him for his great sense of humour -- everybody, that is, except his six-year-old daughter, Jenny.
Recently, one of Jeremy's closest friends asked him to make a speech at a wedding reception.
This is the sort of thing that Jeremy loves.
He prepared the speech carefully and went to the wedding with Jenny.
He had included a large number of funny stories in the speech and, of course, it was a great success.
As soon as he had finished, Jenny told him she wanted to go home.
Jeremy was a little disappointed by this but he did as his daughter asked.
On the way home, he asked Jenny if she had enjoyed the speech.
To his surprise, she said she hadn't.
Jeremy asked her why this was so and she told him that she did not like to see so many people laughing at him!

articles.original_chinese: 杰里米·汉普登交际甚广，是各种聚会上深受大家欢迎的人。人人都钦佩他那绝妙的幽默感——人人，就是说，除他6岁的女儿珍妮之外的每一个人。最近，杰里米的一个最亲密的朋友请他在一个婚礼上祝词。这正是杰里米喜欢做的事情。他认真准备了讲稿，带着珍妮一道去参加了婚礼。他的祝词里面加进了大量逗人的故事，自然大获成功。他刚一讲完，珍妮就对他说她要回家。这不免使杰里米有点扫兴，但他还是按照女儿的要求做了。在回家的路上，他问珍妮是否喜欢他的祝词。使他吃惊的是，她说她不喜欢。杰里米问他为何不喜欢，她说她不愿意看到那么多的人嘲笑他！地址： 38.190.226.110

articles.title: The Channel Tunnel
articles.question: Why was the first tunnel not completed?为什么第一条隧道没有建成？

sentences.text: In 1858, a French engineer, Aimé Thomé de Gamond, arrived in England with a plan for a twenty-one mile tunnel under the English Channel.
He said that it would be possible to build a platform in the centre of the Channel.
This platform would serve as a port and a railway station.
The tunnel would be well-ventilated if tall chimneys were built above sea level.
In 1860, a better plan was put forward by an Englishman, William Low.
He suggested that a double railway tunnel should be built.
This would solve the problem of ventilation, for if a train entered this tunnel, it would draw in fresh air behind it.
Forty-two years later a tunnel was actually begun.
If, at the time, the British had not feared invasion, it would have been completed.The world had to wait almost another 100 years for the Channel Tunnel.
It was officially opened on March 7, 1994, finally connecting Britain to the European continent.

articles.original_chinese: 1858年，一位名叫埃梅·托梅·德·干蒙的法国工程师带着建造一条长21英里、穿越英吉利海陕的隧道计划到了英国。他说，可以在隧道中央建造一座平台，这座平台将用作码头和火车站。如果再建些伸出海面的高大的烟囱状通风管，隧道就具备了良好的通风条件。1860年，一位名叫威廉·洛的英国人提出了一项更好的计划。他提议建一条双轨隧道，这样就解决了通风问题。因为如果有列火车开进隧道，它就把新鲜空气随之抽进了隧道。42年以后，隧道实际已经开始建了。如果不是因为那时英国人害怕入侵，隧道早已建成了。世界不得不再等将近100年才看到海峡隧道竣工。它于1994年3月7日正式开通，将英国与欧洲大陆连到了一起。地址： 38.190.226.110

articles.title: Jumbo versus the police
articles.question: Why did the police have to push Jumbo off the main street?为什么警察不得不把江伯推离主要街道？

sentences.text: Last Christmas, the circus owner, Jimmy Gates, decided to take some presents to a children's hospital.
Dressed up as Father Christmas and accompanied by a 'guard of honour' of six pretty girls, he set off down the main street of the city riding a baby elephant called Jumbo.
He should have known that the police would never allow this sort of thing.
A policeman approached Jimmy and told him he ought to have gone along a side street as Jumbo was holding up the traffic.
Though Jimmy agreed to go at once, Jumbo refused to move.
Fifteen policemen had to push very hard to get him off the main street.
The police had a difficult time, but they were most amused.
'Jumbo must weigh a few tons,' said a policeman afterwards, 'so it was fortunate that we didn't have to carry him.
Of course, we should arrest him, but as he has a good record, we shall let him off this time.'

articles.original_chinese: 去年圣诞节，马戏团老板吉米·盖茨决定送些礼物给儿童医院。他打扮成圣诞老人，在由6个漂亮姑娘组成的“仪仗队”的陪同下，骑上一头名叫江伯的小象，沿着城里的主要街道出发了。他本该知道警察绝不会允许这类事情发生。一个警察走过来告诉吉米，他应该走一条小路，因为江泊阻碍了交通。虽然吉米同意马上就走，但江伯却拒绝移动。15个警察不得不用很大的力气把它推离主要街道。警察虽然吃了苦头，但他们还是感到很有趣。“江伯一定有好几吨重，”一个警察事后这样说，“值得庆幸的是它没让我们抬它走。当然，我们应该逮捕它，但由于它一贯表现很好，这次我们饶了它。”地址： 38.190.226.110

articles.title: Sweet as honey!
articles.question: What was 'sweet as honey' and why?什么东西'像蜜一样甜'，为什么？

sentences.text: In 1963 a Lancaster bomber crashed on Wallis Island, a remote place in the South Pacific, a long way west of Samoa.

The plane wasn't too badly damaged, but over the years, the crash was forgotten and the wreck remained undisturbed.Then in 1989, twenty-six years after the crash, the plane was accidentally rediscovered in an aerial survey of the island.
By this time, a Lancaster bomber in reasonable condition was rare and worth rescuing.
The French authorities had the plane packaged and moved in parts back to France.
Now a group of enthusiasts are going to have the plane restored.
It has four Rolls-Royce Merlin engines, but the group will need to have only three of them rebuilt.
Imagine their surprise and delight when they broke open the packing cases and found that the fourth engine was sweet as honey -- still in perfect condition.
A colony of bees had turned the engine into a hive and it was totally preserved in beeswax!

articles.original_chinese: 1963年，一架兰开斯特轰炸机在瓦立斯岛坠毁。那是南太洋中一个很偏僻的小岛，位于萨摩亚群岛以西，距离群岛还有很长一段距离。飞机损坏的程度并不严重，但是，多年来这起飞机失事已被遗忘，飞机残骸也没受到破坏。于是，到了1989年，飞机失事26年后，在对小岛的一次航空勘查中那架飞机被意外地发现了。到了那个时候，状况良好的兰开斯特轰炸机实属罕见，值得抢救。法国政府让人把飞机包装起来，一部分一部分地搬回法国。一群热心人计划修复这架飞机。该飞机装装配有4台罗尔斯-罗伊斯的默林发动机，但是他们只需要修复其中的3台。想一想他们所感受到的惊奇和兴奋，当他们拆开包装箱时，他们发现第4台发动机就像蜂蜜一样甜——发动机完好无损。一群蜜蜂把发动机当作了蜂房，发动机在蜂蜡中被完整地保存了下来。地址： 38.190.226.110

articles.title: Volcanoes
articles.question: Why does Tazieff risk his life like this?为什么塔捷耶夫要这样冒生命危险？

sentences.text: Haroun Tazieff, the Polish scientist, has spent his lifetime studying active volcanoes and deep caves in all parts of the world.
In 1948, he went to Lake Kivu in the Congo to observe a new volcano which he later named Kituro.
Tazieff was able to set up his camp very close to the volcano while it was erupting violently.
Though he managed to take a number of brilliant photographs, he could not stay near the volcano for very long.
He noticed that a river of liquid rock was coming towards him.
It threatened to surround him completely, but Tazieff managed to escape just in time.
He waited until the volcano became quiet and he was able to return two days later.
This time, he managed to climb into the mouth of Kituro so that he could take photographs and measure temperatures.
Tazieff has often risked his life in this way.
He has been able to tell us more about active volcanoes than any man alive.

articles.original_chinese: 波兰科学家哈罗恩·塔捷耶夫花了毕生的精力来研究世界各地的活火山和探洞。1948年他去了刚果的基伍湖，对一座后来被他命名为基图罗的新火山进行观察。当火山正在猛烈地喷发时，塔捷耶夫有办法把帐篷搭在离它非常近的地方。尽管他设法拍了一些十分精彩的照片，但他却不能在火山附近停留太长的时间。他发现有一股岩浆正向他流过来，眼看就要将他团团围住，但塔捷耶夫还是设法及时逃离了。他等到火山平静下来，两天以后又返回去。这次他设法爬进了基图罗火山口，以便能拍摄照片和测试温度。塔捷耶夫经常冒这样的生命危险。他能告诉我们的有关活火山的情况比任何在世的人都要多。地址： 38.190.226.110

articles.title: Persistent
articles.question: Why did Elizabeth tell Nigel that she was going to the dentist?为什么伊丽莎白告诉奈杰尔她要去牙医那里？

sentences.text: I crossed the street to avoid meeting him, but he saw me and came running towards me.
It was no use pretending that I had not seen him, so I waved to him.
I never enjoy meeting Bert Dykes.
He never has anything to do.
No matter how busy you are, he always insists on coming with you.
I had to think of a way of preventing him from following me around all morning.
'Hello, Bert,' I said.
'Fancy meeting you here!' 'Hi, Elizabeth,' Bert answered.
'I was just wondering how to spend the morning -- until I saw you.
You're not busy doing anything, are you?' 'No, not at all,' I answered.
'I'm going to ...' 'Would you mind my coming with you?' he asked, before I had finished speaking.
'Not at all,' I lied, 'but I'm going to the dentist.' 'Then I'll come with you,' he answered.
'There's always plenty to read in the waiting room!'

articles.original_chinese: 我穿过马路以便避开他，但他看到我并朝我跑过来。若再装作没看见他已是没有用了，我只好向他招手。我就怕遇到奈杰尔·戴克斯。他从来都是无事可做，不管你多忙，他总是坚持要跟你去。我得想办法不让他整个上午缠着我。“你好，奈杰尔，想不到在这儿见到你。”我说。“你好，伊丽莎白，”奈杰尔回答说，“我正不知道怎么消磨这一上午呢，正好见到好。你不忙，是吗？”“不，不忙，我打算去......”我回答。“我跟你一道去行吗？”没等我说完话他就问道。“没关系，但我准备去牙医那里。”我说了个谎。“那我也跟你去，候诊室里总有很多东西可供阅读！”他回答。地址： 38.190.226.110

articles.title: But not murder!
articles.question: Do you think that the writer passed his driving test? Why?你认为作者通过驾驶考试了吗？为什么？

sentences.text: I was being tested for a driving licence for the third time.
I had been asked to drive in heavy traffic and had done so successfully.
After having been instructed to drive out of town, I began to acquire confidence.
Sure that I had passed, I was almost beginning to enjoy my test.
The examiner must have been pleased with my performance, for he smiled and said, 'Just one more thing, Mr Eames.
Let us suppose that a child suddenly crosses the road in front of you.
As soon as I tap on the window, I want the car to be stopped immediately.' I continued driving and after some time, the examiner tapped loudly.
Though the sound could be heard clearly, it took me a long time to react.
I suddenly pressed the brake pedal hard and we were both thrown forward.
The examiner looked at me sadly.
'Mr Eames,' he said, in a mournful voice, 'you have just killed that child!'

articles.original_chinese: 我第3次接受驾驶执照考试。按照要求在车辆拥挤的路上驾驶，我圆满地完成了。在接到把车开出城的指令后，我开始有了信心。确信我已通过考试，所以我几乎开始喜欢起这次考试。主考人对我的驾驶想必是满意的，因为他微笑着说：“埃姆斯先生，只剩1项了。让我们假设一个小孩子突然在你前面穿过马路。我一敲车窗，你必须把车停在5英尺之内。”我继续往前开着。过了一会儿，主考人砰砰地敲了起来。虽然声音听得很清楚，但我过了好一会儿才作出反应。我突然用力踩紧刹车踏板，结果我俩的身体都向前冲去。主考人伤心地看着我。“埃姆斯先生，”他以悲伤的声调说，“你刚刚把那个小孩压死了！”地址： 38.190.226.110

articles.title: Red for danger
articles.question: How was the drunk removed from the ring?醉汉是如何被带离斗牛场的？

sentences.text: During a bullfight, a drunk suddenly wandered into the middle of the ring.
The crowd began to shout, but the drunk was unaware of the danger.
The bull was busy with the matador at the time, but it suddenly caught sight of the drunk who was shouting rude remarks and waving a red cap.
Apparently sensitive to criticism, the bull forgot all about the matador and charged at the drunk.
The crowd suddenly grew quiet.
The drunk, however, seemed quite sure of himself.
When the bull got close to him, he clumsily stepped aside to let it pass.
The crowd broke into cheers and the drunk bowed.
By this time, however, three men had come into the ring and they quickly dragged the drunk to safety.
Even the bull seemed to feel sorry for him, for it looked on sympathetically until the drunk was out of the way before once more turning its attention to the matador.

articles.original_chinese: 在一次斗牛时，一个醉汉突然溜达到斗牛场中间，人们开始大叫起来，但醉汉却没有意识到危险。当时那公牛正忙于对付斗牛士，但突然它看见了醉汉，只见他正大声说着粗鲁的话，手里挥动着一顶红帽子。对挑衅显然非常敏感的公牛完全撇开斗牛士，直奔醉汉而来。观众突然静了下来，可这醉汉像是很有把握似的。当公牛逼近他时，他踉跄地住旁边一闪，牛扑空了。观众欢呼起来，醉汉向人们鞠躬致谢。然而，此时已有3个人进入斗牛场，迅速把醉汉拉到安全的地方。好像连牛也在为他感到遗憾，因为它一直同情地看着醉汉，直到他的背影消逝，才重新将注意力转向斗牛士。地址： 38.190.226.110

articles.title: A famous clock
articles.question: Has Big Ben ever gone wrong?大本钟出过故障吗？

sentences.text: When you visit London, one of the first things you will see is Big Ben, the famous clock which can be heard all over the world on the B.B.C.
If the Houses of Parliament had not been burned down in 1834, the great clock would never have been erected.
Big Ben takes its name from Sir Benjamin Hall who was responsible for the making of the clock when the new Houses of Parliament were being built.
It is not only of immense size, but is extremely accurate as well.
Officials from Greenwich Observatory have the clock checked twice a day.
On the B.B.C. you can hear the clock when it is actually striking because microphones are connected to the clock tower.
Big Ben has rarely gone wrong.
Once, however, it failed to give the correct time.
A painter who had been working on the tower hung a pot of paint on one of the hands and slowed it down!

articles.original_chinese: 当你游览伦敦时，首先看到的东西之一就是“大笨钟”，即那座从英国广播公司的广播中全世界都可以听到它的声音的著名大钟。如果不是国会大厦在1834年被焚毁的话，这座大钟永远也不会建造。“大笨钟”得名于本杰明·霍尔爵士，因为当建造新的国会大厦时，他负责建造大钟。此钟不仅外型巨大，而且走时也非常准确。格林尼治天文台的官员们每天两次派人矫正此钟。当大钟打点的时候，你可以从英国广播公司的广播中听到，因为钟塔上接了麦克风。“大笨钟”很少出差错。然而有一次，它却把时间报错。在钟塔上干活的一位油漆工把一只油漆桶挂在了一根指针上，把钟弄慢了！地址： 38.190.226.110

articles.title: A car called Bluebird
articles.question: What mistake was made?出了什么差错？

sentences.text: The great racing driver, Sir Malcolm Campbell, was the first man to drive at over 300 miles per hour.
He set up a new world record in September 1935 at Bonneville Salt Flats, Utah.
Bluebird, the car he was driving, had been specially built for him.
It was over 30 feet in length and had a 2,500-horsepower engine.
Although Campbell reached a speed of over 304 miles per hour, he had great difficulty in controlling the car because a tyre burst during the first run.
After his attempt, Campbell was disappointed to learn that his average speed had been 299 miles per hour.
However, a few days later, he was told that a mistake had been made.
His average speed had been 301 miles per hour.
Since that time, racing drivers have reached speeds of over 400 miles an hour.
Following his father's footsteps many years later, Sir Malcolm's son, Donald, also set up a world record.
Like his father, he was driving a car called Bluebird.

articles.original_chinese: 杰出的赛车选手马尔科姆·坎贝尔爵士是第一个以每小时超过300英里的速度驾车的人。他于1935年9月在犹他州的邦纳维尔盐滩创造了一项新的世界纪录。他驾驶的“蓝鸟”牌汽车是专门为他制造的。它的车身长30英尺，有一个2,500 马力的发动机。尽管坎贝尔达到了每小时超过304英里的速度，但他很难把汽车控制住，因为在开始的行程中爆了一只轮胎。比赛结束后，坎贝尔非常失望地得知他的平均时速是299英里。然而，几天之后，有人告诉他说弄错了。他的平均时速实际是301英里。从那时以来，赛车选手已达到每小时600英里的速度。很多年之后，马尔科姆爵士的儿子唐纳德踏着父亲的足迹，也创造了一项世界纪录。同他父亲一样，他也驾驶着一辆名叫“蓝鸟”的汽车。地址： 38.190.226.110

sentences.text: Little boys who play truant from school are unimaginative.
sentences.translation: 逃学的孩子们都缺乏想像力。

sentences.text: A quiet day's fishing, or eight hours in a cinema seeing the same film over and over again, is usually as far as they get.
sentences.translation: 他们通常能够做到的，至多也就是安静地钓上一天鱼，或在电影院里坐上8个小时，一遍遍地看同一部电影。

sentences.text: They have all been put to shame by a boy who, while playing truant, travelled 1,600 miles.
sentences.translation: 而有那么一个小男孩，他在逃学期间旅行了1,600英里，从而使上述所有逃学的孩子们都相形见绌了。

sentences.text: He hitchhiked to Dover and, towards evening, went into a boat to find somewhere to sleep.
sentences.translation: 他搭便车到了多佛，天快黑时钻进了一条船，想找个地方睡觉。

sentences.text: When he woke up next morning, he discovered that the boat had, in the meantime, travelled to Calais.
sentences.translation: 第二天早上他醒来时，发现船在这段时间已经到了加。

sentences.text: No one noticed the boy as he crept off.
sentences.translation: 当男孩从船里爬出来时，谁也没有发现他。

sentences.text: From there, he hitchhiked to Paris in a lorry.
sentences.translation: 从那里他又搭上卡车到了巴黎。

sentences.text: The driver gave him a few biscuits and a cup of coffee and left him just outside the city.
sentences.translation: 司机给了他几块饼干和一杯咖啡，就把他丢在了城外。

sentences.text: The next car the boy stopped did not take him into the centre of Paris as he hoped it would, but to Perpignan on the French-Spanish border.
sentences.translation: 男孩截住的下一辆车，没有像他希望的那样把他带到巴黎市中心，而是把他带到了法国和西班牙边界上的佩皮尼昂。

sentences.text: There he was picked up by a policeman and sent back to England by the local authorities.
sentences.translation: 他在那儿被一个警察抓住了，之后被当局送回了英国。

sentences.text: He has surely set up a record for the thousands of boys who dream of evading school.
sentences.translation: 他无疑为成千上万梦想逃避上学的孩子们创造了一项纪录。

sentences.text: An ancient bus stopped by a dry river bed and a party of famous actors and actresses got off.
sentences.translation: 一辆古旧的汽车停在一条干涸的河床边，一群著名男女演员下了车。

sentences.text: Dressed in dark glasses and old clothes, they had taken special precautions so that no one should recognize them.
sentences.translation: 他们戴着墨镜，穿着旧衣裳，特别小心以防别人认出他们。

sentences.text: But as they soon discovered, disguises can sometimes be too perfect.
sentences.translation: 但他们很快就发觉，化装的效果有时过分完美了。

sentences.text: 'This is a wonderful place for a picnic,' said Gloria Gleam.
sentences.translation: “在这个地方野餐简直太妙了，”格格利亚·格利姆说。

sentences.text: 'It couldn't be better, Gloria,' Brinksley Meers agreed.
sentences.translation: “是再好不过的了，格格利亚。

sentences.text: 'No newspaper men, no film fans!
sentences.translation: ”布林克斯利·米尔斯表示同意，“没有记者，没有影迷！

sentences.text: Why don't we come more often?' Meanwhile, two other actors, Rockwall Slinger and Merlin Greeves, had carried two large food baskets to a shady spot under some trees.
sentences.translation: 我们为什么不经常来这里呢？

sentences.text: When they had all made themselves comfortable, a stranger appeared.
sentences.translation: ”此时，另外两位演员，罗克沃尔·斯林格和默林·格里夫斯，已经把两个大食品篮子提到了一片树荫下。

sentences.text: He looked very angry.
sentences.translation: 当他们都已安排舒适时，一个陌生人出现了。

sentences.text: 'Now you get out of here, all of you!' he shouted.
sentences.translation: 他看上去非常气愤。

sentences.text: 'I'm sheriff here.
sentences.translation: “你们都从这里走开，全都走开！

sentences.text: Do you see that notice?
sentences.translation: ”他大叫着，“我是这里的司法长官。

sentences.text: It says "No Camping" -- in case you can't read!' 'Look, sheriff,' said Rockwall, 'don't be too hard on us.
sentences.translation: 你们看到那个布告牌了吗？

sentences.text: I'm Rockwall Slinger and this is Merlin Greeves.' 'Oh, is it?' said the sheriff with a sneer.
sentences.translation: 上面写着‘禁止野营’——除非你们不识字！

sentences.text: 'Well, I'm Brinksley Meers, and my other name is Gloria Gleam.
sentences.translation: ”“好了，好了，司法官，”罗克沃尔说，“别使我们难堪。

sentences.text: Now you get out of here fast!'
sentences.translation: 我是罗克沃尔·斯林格，这位是默林·格里夫斯。

sentences.text: When a light passenger plane flew off course some time ago, it crashed in the mountains and its pilot was killed.
sentences.translation: 不久前，一架轻型客机偏离了航线，在山区坠毁，飞行员丧生。

sentences.text: The only passengers, a young woman and her two baby daughters, were unhurt.
sentences.translation: 机上仅有的乘客，一位年轻的妇女和她的两个女婴却平安无事。

sentences.text: It was the middle of winter.
sentences.translation: 此时正值隆冬季节，地上积着厚厚的雪。

sentences.text: Snow lay thick on the ground.
sentences.translation: 这位妇女知道，即使最近的村庄也有数英里远。

sentences.text: The woman knew that the nearest village was miles away.
sentences.translation: 天黑下来的时候，她把提箱当作小床，把两个孩子放了进去，又把所有能找到的衣服都盖在了孩子们身上。

sentences.text: When it grew dark, she turned a suitcase into a bed and put the children inside it, covering them with all the clothes she could find.
sentences.translation: 夜里，天冷得厉害。

sentences.text: During the night, it got terribly cold.
sentences.translation: 这位妇女尽可能地靠近孩子，甚至自己也想钻进箱子里去，只是箱子太小了。

sentences.text: The woman kept as near as she could to the children and even tried to get into the case herself, but it was too small.
sentences.translation: 第二天一大早，她听到头顶上有飞机飞过，但不知道怎样才能发个信号。

sentences.text: Early next morning, she heard planes passing overhead and wondered how she could send a signal.
sentences.translation: 后来她有了一个主意。

sentences.text: Then she had an idea.
sentences.translation: 她在雪地上踩出了“SOS”这3个字母。

sentences.text: She stamped out the letters 'SOS' in the snow.
sentences.translation: 幸运得很，一位飞行员看到这个信号，用无线电给最近的城镇发了报。

sentences.text: Fortunately, a pilot saw the signal and sent a message by radio to the nearest town.
sentences.translation: 不久，一架直升飞机飞抵飞机失事现场，来搭救这几个幸存者。

sentences.text: It was not long before a helicopter arrived on the scene to rescue the survivors of the plane crash.
'To end our special news bulletin,' said the voice of the television announcer, 'we are taking you to the macaroni fields of Calabria.
sentences.translation: “作为我们专题新闻节目的结尾，”电视广播员说，“我们现在到克拉布利亚的通心粉田里。

sentences.text: Macaroni has been grown in this area for over six hundred years.
sentences.translation: 通心粉在这个地区已经种植了600多年了。

sentences.text: Two of the leading growers, Giuseppe Moldova and Riccardo Brabante, tell me that they have been expecting a splendid crop this year and harvesting has begun earlier than usual.
sentences.translation: 两个主要种植者，朱塞皮·莫尔道瓦和里卡多·布拉班特告诉我，他们一直期待着今年获得一个大丰收，收割工作比往年开始要早些。

sentences.text: Here you can see two workers who, between them, have just finished cutting three cartloads of golden brown macaroni stalks.
sentences.translation: 这里您可以看到两个工人，他们协力割下了3车金黄色的通心粉秸。

sentences.text: The whole village has been working day and night gathering and threshing this year's crop before the September rains.
sentences.translation: 全村的人都日夜奋战，要赶在9月的雨季之前把今年的庄稼收获上来，打完场。

sentences.text: On the right, you can see Mrs Brabante herself.
sentences.translation: 在屏幕的右侧，您可以看到布拉班特太太本人，她已经帮了她的丈夫30年了。

sentences.text: She has been helping her husband for thirty years now.
sentences.translation: 布拉班特太太现在正和负责通心粉加工的当地加工厂的经理交谈。

sentences.text: Mrs Brabante is talking to the manager of the local factory where the crop is processed.
sentences.translation: 这最后一个镜头向您展示了收获之后将发生的事情：著名的克拉布利亚人吃通心粉大赛！

sentences.text: This last scene shows you what will happen at the end of the harvest: the famous Calabrian macaroni-eating competition!
sentences.translation: 目前的冠军弗拉特里先生，自1991年以来，年年获胜。

sentences.text: Signor Fratelli, the present champion, has won it every year since 1961.
sentences.translation: 今天——4月1日，星期四——的专题新闻节目到此结束。

sentences.text: And that ends our special bulletin for today, Thursday, April lst.
sentences.translation: 现在我们回到电视演播室。

sentences.text: We are now returning you to the studio.'
The mummy of an Egyptian woman who died in 800 B.C. has just had an operation.
sentences.translation: 死于公元前800年的一位埃及妇女的木乃伊刚刚接受了一次手术。

sentences.text: The mummy is that of Shepenmut who was once a singer in the Temple of Thebes.
sentences.translation: 这是曾在底比斯神殿里当过歌手的赛潘姆特的木乃伊。

sentences.text: As there were strange marks on the X-ray plates taken of the mummy, doctors have been trying to find out whether the woman died of a rare disease.
sentences.translation: 由于在给这个木乃伊拍摄的X光片子上有点奇怪的斑点，所以，医生们一直试图搞清这位妇女是否死于一种罕见的疾病。

sentences.text: The only way to do this was to operate.
sentences.translation: 搞清的唯一办法就是手术。

sentences.text: The operation, which lasted for over four hours, proved to be very difficult because of the hard resin which covered the skin.
sentences.translation: 手术持续了4个多小时，非常难做，因为皮肤上覆盖着一层硬硬的树脂。

sentences.text: The doctors removed a section of the mummy and sent it to a laboratory.
sentences.translation: 医生们从木乃伊身上取下一个切片，送去化验。

sentences.text: They also found something which the X-ray plates did not show: a small wax figure of the god Duamutef.
sentences.translation: 他们还发现了X光片所没有显示的东西：一个蜡制的杜瓦木特夫神小塑像。

sentences.text: This god which has the head of a cow was normally placed inside a mummy.
sentences.translation: 这种牛头人身的神像通常被放在木乃伊体内。

sentences.text: The doctors have not yet decided how the woman died.
sentences.translation: 医生们至今还未确定这位妇女的死因。

sentences.text: They feared that the mummy would fall to pieces when they cut it open, but fortunately this has not happened.
sentences.translation: 他们曾担心在把木乃伊切开后，它会散成碎片，但幸运得很，这种情况并未发生。

sentences.text: The mummy successfully survived the operation.
sentences.translation: 这具木乃伊成功地经受了这次手术。

sentences.text: After reading an article entitled 'Cigarette Smoking and Your Health' I lit a cigarette to calm my nerves.
sentences.translation: 读完一篇题为《吸烟与健康》的文章之后，我点上了一枝香烟，来镇定一下自己紧张的神经。

sentences.text: I smoked with concentration and pleasure as I was sure that this would be my last cigarette.
sentences.translation: 我聚精会神而又愉快地吸着这枝烟。

sentences.text: For a whole week I did not smoke at all and during this time, my wife suffered terribly.
sentences.translation: 因为我确信这是我最后一枝烟了。

sentences.text: I had all the usual symptoms of someone giving up smoking: a bad temper and an enormous appetite.
sentences.translation: 整整一个星期我根本没有吸烟。

sentences.text: My friends kept on offering me cigarettes and cigars.
sentences.translation: 在此期间，我妻子吃尽了苦头。

sentences.text: They made no effort to hide their amusement whenever I produced a packet of sweets from my pocket.
sentences.translation: 我具备了戒烟者通常表现出来的所有症状：脾气暴躁和食欲旺盛。

sentences.text: After seven days of this I went to a party.
sentences.translation: 我的朋友们不断地向我递香烟和雪茄。

sentences.text: Everybody around me was smoking and I felt extremely uncomfortable.
sentences.translation: 每当我从口袋里掏出一包糖果时，他们都毫不掩饰地表现出他们对此感到非常好笑。

sentences.text: When my old friend Brian urged me to accept a cigarette, it was more than I could bear.
sentences.translation: 这样过了7天以后，我去参加一次聚会。

sentences.text: I took one guiltily, lit it and smoked with satisfaction.
sentences.translation: 我周围的每个人都在吸烟，我感到非常不自在。

sentences.text: My wife was delighted that things had returned to normal once more.
sentences.translation: 当我的老朋友布赖恩极力劝我接受一枝香烟时，我再也忍不住了。

sentences.text: Anyway, as Brian pointed out, it is the easiest thing in the world to give up smoking.
sentences.translation: 我内疚地接过一枝点上，心满意足地抽起来。

sentences.text: He himself has done it lots of times!
sentences.translation: 一切又都恢复了正常，为此我妻子十分高兴。

sentences.text: I used to travel by air a great deal when I was a boy.
sentences.translation: 我在幼年的时候，曾多次乘飞机旅行。

sentences.text: My parents used to live in South America and I used to fly there from Europe in the holidays.
sentences.translation: 我的父母曾经住在南美洲，所以假期里我常从欧洲乘飞机到他们那里。

sentences.text: An air hostess would take charge of me and I never had an unpleasant experience.
sentences.translation: 我总是由一位空中乘务员照管，从未遇到过不愉快的经历。

sentences.text: I am used to travelling by air and only on one occasion have I ever felt frightened.
sentences.translation: 我习惯了乘飞机旅行，只是有一次把我吓坏了。

sentences.text: After taking off, we were flying low over the city and slowly gaining height, when the plane suddenly turned round and flew back to the airport.
sentences.translation: 起飞之后，我们在城市上空低低地飞行，然后慢慢爬高。

sentences.text: While we were waiting to land, an air hostess told us to keep calm and to get off the plane quietly as soon as it had touched down.
sentences.translation: 这时飞机突然调转头来，飞回了机场。

sentences.text: Everybody on board was worried and we were curious to find out what had happened.
sentences.translation: 在我们等待降落时，一位空中乘务员告诉我们要保持镇静，待飞机一着陆，就马上不声不响地离开飞机。

sentences.text: Later we learnt that there was a very important person on board.
sentences.translation: 飞机上的人都很着急，大家都急于想知道究竟出了什么事。

sentences.text: The police had been told that a bomb had been planted on the plane.
sentences.translation: 后来我们才得知，飞机上坐了一位非常重要的人物。

sentences.text: After we had landed, the plane was searched thoroughly.
sentences.translation: 有人报告警察，说飞机上安放了一枚炸弹。

sentences.text: Fortunately, nothing was found and five hours later we were able to take off again.
sentences.translation: 我们降落之后，飞机被彻底搜查了一遍。

sentences.text: Perhaps the most extraordinary building of the nineteenth century was the Crystal Palace, which was built in Hyde Park for the Great Exhibition of 1851.
sentences.translation: 19世纪最不寻常的建筑也许要数水晶宫了，它是为1851年的“世界博览会”而建在海德公园的。

sentences.text: The Crystal Palace was different from all other buildings in the world, for it was made of iron and glass.
sentences.translation: 这座水晶宫不同于世界上所有的其他建筑，因为它是用钢和玻璃建成的。

sentences.text: It was one of the biggest buildings of all time and a lot of people from many countries came to see it.
sentences.translation: 它是有史以来最高大的建筑物之一，因此，人们从各个国家纷纷前来参观。

sentences.text: A great many goods were sent to the exhibition from various parts of the world.
sentences.translation: 大量的商品从世界各地运送到了博览会，参展的还有很多机器，其中最奇妙的是内史密斯的蒸汽锤。

sentences.text: There was also a great deal of machinery on display.
sentences.translation: 尽管在当时旅行不像现在这么容易，但汽船还是把成千上万的参观者从欧洲大陆送过了英吉利海峡。

sentences.text: The most wonderful piece of machinery on show was Nasmyth's steam hammer.
sentences.translation: 一到英国，火车就把他们送到了水晶宫。

sentences.text: Though in those days, travelling was not as easy as it is today, steam boats carried thousands of visitors across the Channel from Europe.
sentences.translation: 参观的人数总共是600万。

sentences.text: On arriving in England, they were taken to the Crystal Palace by train.
sentences.translation: 博览会的赢利用来建造博物馆和高等学校。

sentences.text: There were six million visitors in all, and the profits from the exhibition were used to build museums and colleges.
sentences.translation: 后来，“水晶宫”被移到了伦敦南部。

sentences.text: Later, the Crystal Palace was moved to South London.
sentences.translation: 在1936年被焚毁之前，它一直是世界上最著名的建筑物之一。

sentences.text: It remained one of the most famous buildings in the world until it was burnt down in 1936.
When he had killed the guard, the prisoner of war quickly dragged him into the bushes.
sentences.translation: 那个战俘杀死卫兵以后，迅速地把尸体拖进了灌木丛。

sentences.text: Working rapidly in the darkness, he soon changed into the dead man's clothes.
sentences.translation: 他在黑暗中忙活了一阵儿，很快就换上了死者的衣服。

sentences.text: Now, dressed in a blue uniform and with a rifle over his shoulder, the prisoner marched boldly up and down in front of the camp.
sentences.translation: 现在他身穿蓝军装，肩扛步枪，在军营门前大胆地来回走看。

sentences.text: He could hear shouting in the camp itself.
sentences.translation: 他听得军营里面的喧闹声。

sentences.text: Lights were blazing and men were running here and there: they had just discovered that a prisoner had escaped.
sentences.translation: 那里灯米通明，人们在东奔西跑：他们刚刚发现有一个俘虏跑了。

sentences.text: At that moment, a large black car with four officers inside it, stopped at the camp gates.
sentences.translation: 正在此时，一辆黑色大轿车在军营门口停了下来。

sentences.text: The officers got out and the prisoner stood to attention and saluted as they passed.
sentences.translation: 里面坐了4个军官。

sentences.text: When they had gone, the driver of the car came towards him.
sentences.translation: 军官们下了车，战俘立正站好，并在他们从他面前经过时敬了礼。

sentences.text: The man obviously wanted to talk.
sentences.translation: 他们走后，汽车司机向他走来，这人显然是想聊天。

sentences.text: He was rather elderly with grey hair and clear blue eyes.
sentences.translation: 他上了年纪，有着灰白的头发和明亮的蓝眼睛。

sentences.text: The prisoner felt sorry for him, but there was nothing else he could do.
sentences.translation: 战俘为他感到惋惜，但却没有别的选择。

sentences.text: As the man came near, the prisoner knocked him to the ground with a sharp blow.
sentences.translation: 当这个人走近时，战俘一拳把他打倒在地，然后跳进车里，以最快的速度把车开走了。

sentences.text: Then, jumping into the car, he drove off as quickly as he could.
Fishermen and sailors sometimes claim to have seen monsters in the sea.
sentences.translation: 渔夫和水手们有时声称自己看到过海里的妖怪。

sentences.text: Though people have often laughed at stories told by seamen, it is now known that many of these 'monsters' which have at times been sighted are simply strange fish.
sentences.translation: 虽然人们常常对水手们讲的故事付诸一笑， 但现在看来，人们有时看到的这些“妖怪”很多不过是些奇怪的鱼。

sentences.text: Occasionally, unusual creatures are washed to the shore, but they are rarely caught out at sea.
sentences.translation: 一些异常的生物偶尔会被冲到岸上来，但它们在海上却极少能被捕到。

sentences.text: Some time ago, however, a peculiar fish was caught near Madagascar.
sentences.translation: 然而不久前，在马达加斯加附近的海里却捕到了一条奇怪的鱼。

sentences.text: A small fishing boat was carried miles out to sea by the powerful fish as it pulled on the line.
sentences.translation: 一条小渔船被一条咬住钩的强壮的大鱼拖到了几英里以外的海面上。

sentences.text: Realizing that this was no ordinary fish, the fisherman made every effort not to damage it in any way.
sentences.translation: 那位渔民意识到这根本不是一条普通的鱼，于是千方百计不让它受到丝毫伤害。

sentences.text: When it was eventually brought to shore, it was found to be over thirteen feet long.
sentences.translation: 当终于把它弄上岸后，人们发现它身长超过了13英尺。

sentences.text: It had a head like a horse, big blue eyes, shining silver skin, and a bright red tail.
sentences.translation: 它长着一个像马一样的头，有着大的蓝眼睛和闪闪发光的银色皮肤，还有一条鲜红色的尾巴。

sentences.text: The fish, which has since been sent to a museum where it is being examined by a scientist, is called an oarfish.
sentences.translation: 此鱼叫桨鱼，被送进了博物馆，现正接受一位科学家的检查。

sentences.text: Such creatures have rarely been seen alive by man as they live at a depth of six hundred feet.
sentences.translation: 人们很少能看到活着的这类动物，因为它们生活在600英尺深的水下。

sentences.text: The former Prime Minister, Mr Wentworth Lane, was defeated in the recent elections.
sentences.translation: 前首相温特沃兹·莱恩先生在最近的大选中被击败。

sentences.text: He is now retiring from political life and has gone abroad.
sentences.translation: 他现在退出了政界，到国外去了。

sentences.text: My friend, Crawley, has always been a fanatical opponent of Mr Lane's Radical Progressive Party.
sentences.translation: 我的朋友帕特里克一直是莱恩先生的激进党的强烈反对者。

sentences.text: After the elections, Crawley went to the former Prime Minister's house.
sentences.translation: 大选结束后，帕特里克来到了前首相的住处。

sentences.text: When he asked if Mr Lane lived there, the policeman on duty told him that since his defeat, the ex-Prime Minister had gone abroad.
sentences.translation: 当他询问莱恩先生是否住在那里时，值班的警察告诉他这位前首相落选后出国去了。

sentences.text: On the following day, Crawley went to the house again.
sentences.translation: 第二天，帕特里克再次来到首相的住处。

sentences.text: The same policeman was just walking slowly past the entrance, when Crawley asked the same question.
sentences.translation: 昨天的那位警察正从门口慢慢走过，帕特里克上前问了和昨天同样的问题。

sentences.text: Though a little suspicious this time, the policeman gave him the same answer.
sentences.translation: 虽然那位警察这次有点疑心，但还是对他作了同样的回答。

sentences.text: The day after, Crawley went to the house once more and asked exactly the same question.
sentences.translation: 第三天，帕特里克又去了，提出了同前两天完全一样的问题。

sentences.text: This time, the policeman lost his temper.
sentences.translation: 这一次警察火了。

sentences.text: 'I told you yesterday and the day before yesterday,' he shouted, 'Mr Lane was defeated in the elections.
sentences.translation: “我昨天和前天都告诉过您了，”他大叫着，“莱恩先生在大选中被击败了，他已经退出了政界去国外了！

sentences.text: He has retired from political life and gone to live abroad!' 'I know,' answered Crawley, 'but I love to hear you say it!'
sentences.translation: ”“这我都知道，”帕特里克说，“可我就是喜欢听你说出来！

sentences.text: Busmen have decided to go on strike next week.
sentences.translation: 公共汽车司机决定下星期罢工。

sentences.text: The strike is due to begin on Tuesday.
sentences.translation: 罢工定于星期二开始，谁也不知道会持续多久。

sentences.text: No one knows how long it will last.
sentences.translation: 司机们声称此次罢工将一直持续到就工资和工作条件问题达成全面协议的时候为止。

sentences.text: The busmen have stated that the strike will continue until general agreement is reached about pay and working conditions.
sentences.translation: 多数人认为此次罢工至少会持续一个星期。

sentences.text: Most people believe that the strike will last for at least a week.
sentences.translation: 很多私人汽车的车主正准备为乘车上班的人们提供“免费乘车”的服务，这将在某种程度上减轻对火车的压力。

sentences.text: Many owners of private cars are going to offer 'free rides' to people on their way to work.
sentences.translation: 与此同时，有一部分大学生自愿在罢工期间驾驶公共汽车。

sentences.text: This will relieve pressure on the trains to some extent.
sentences.translation: 所有的学生都是开车的能手，但在驾驶公共汽车之前，他们必须通过一项专门测验。

sentences.text: Meanwhile, a number of university students have volunteered to drive buses while the strike lasts.
sentences.translation: 学生们准备在两天后就接受测验。

sentences.text: All the young men are expert drivers, but before they drive any of the buses, they will have to pass a special test.
sentences.translation: 即使这样，人们仍会感到上班有困难。

sentences.text: The students are going to take the test in two days' time.
sentences.translation: 但到目前为止，公众已经向新闻界写信表达他们对学生们的感激之情了。

sentences.text: Even so, people are going to find it difficult to get to work.
sentences.translation: 只有一两个人提出反对意见，说学生们会把车开得太快！

sentences.text: But so far, the public has expressed its gratitude to the students in letters to the press.
Only one or two people have objected that the students will drive too fast!
I have just received a letter from my old school informing me that my former headmaster, Mr Reginald Page, will be retiring next week.
sentences.translation: 我刚刚收到母校的一封信，通知我说以前的校长斯图亚特·佩奇先生下星期就退休了。

sentences.text: Pupils of the school, old and new, will be sending him a present to mark the occasion.
sentences.translation: 为了纪念这个日子，学校的学生——无论老同学还是新同学——将送他一件礼物。

sentences.text: All those who have contributed towards the gift will sign their names in a large album which will be sent to the headmaster's home.
sentences.translation: 所有凑钱买此礼品的人都将自己的名字签在一本大签名簿上，签名簿将被送到校长的家里。

sentences.text: We shall all remember Mr Page for his patience and understanding and for the kindly encouragement he gave us when we went so unwillingly to school.
sentences.translation: 我们不会忘记佩奇先生对我们既有耐心又充满理解， 也不会忘记在我们不愿去上学时他给予我们的亲切鼓励。

sentences.text: A great many former pupils will be attending a farewell dinner in his honour next Thursday.
sentences.translation: 很多老同学都准备参加下星期四为他举行的告别宴会。

sentences.text: It is a curious coincidence that the day before his retirement, Mr Page will have been teaching for a total of forty years.
sentences.translation: 佩奇先生退休的前一天正好是他执教满40年的日子，这真是奇妙的巧合。

sentences.text: After he has retired, he will devote himself to gardening.
sentences.translation: 他退休后，将致力于园艺。

sentences.text: For him, this will be an entirely new hobby.
sentences.translation: 对于他来说，这将是一种全新的爱好。

sentences.text: But this does not matter, for, as he has often remarked, one is never too old to learn.
sentences.translation: 但这没有关系，因为正如他常说的那样，人要活到老学到老

sentences.text: As the man tried to swing the speedboat round, the steering wheel came away in his hands.
sentences.translation: 当那人试图让快艇转弯时，方向盘脱手了。

sentences.text: He waved desperately to his companion, who had been water skiing for the last fifteen minutes.
sentences.translation: 他绝望地向他的伙伴挥手，他的伙伴在过去的15分钟里一直在滑水。

sentences.text: Both men had hardly had time to realize what was happening when they were thrown violently into the sea.
sentences.translation: 他们两个还没来得及意识到究竟发生了什么事情，就被猛地抛入了海里。

sentences.text: The speedboat had struck a buoy, but it continued to move very quickly across the water.
sentences.translation: 快艇撞上了一个浮标，但它仍在水面上快速行驶着。

sentences.text: Both men had just begun to swim towards the shore, when they noticed with dismay that the speedboat was moving in a circle.
sentences.translation: 两个人刚开始向岸边游去，就突然惊愕地发现快艇正在转着圈行驶， 它现在正以惊人的速度直冲他们驶来。

sentences.text: It now came straight towards them at tremendous speed.
sentences.translation: 不到1分钟的工夫，它从离他们只有几英尺远的地方呼啸着驶了过去。

sentences.text: In less than a minute, it roared past them only a few feet away.
sentences.translation: 快艇过去之后， 他们以最快的速度向前游去， 因为他们知道快艇马上就要转回来。

sentences.text: After it had passed, they swam on as quickly as they could because they knew that the boat would soon return.
sentences.translation: 他们刚刚来得及游出危险区， 快艇就又转完了一圈。

sentences.text: They had just had enough time to swim out of danger when the boat again completed a circle.
sentences.translation: 然而这一次它的速度慢多了。

sentences.text: On this occasion, however, it had slowed down considerably.
sentences.translation: 汽油几乎已经用光。

sentences.text: The petrol had nearly all been used up.
sentences.translation: 没过多久，噪音便彻底消失，快艇开始在水面上慢悠悠地漂流。

sentences.text: Before long, the noise dropped completely and the boat began to drift gently across the water.
'At the time the murder was committed, I was travelling on the 8 o'clock train to London,' said the man.
sentences.translation: “在凶杀发生的时候，我正坐在8点钟开往伦敦的火车上。

sentences.text: 'Do you always catch such an early train?' asked the inspector.
sentences.translation: ”那人说。

sentences.text: 'Of course I do,' answered the man.
sentences.translation: “您总是赶这样早的火车？

sentences.text: 'I must be at work at 10 o'clock.
sentences.translation: ”探长问。

sentences.text: My employer will confirm that I was there on time.' 'Would a later train get you to work on time?' asked the inspector.
sentences.translation: “当然是的，”那人回答。

sentences.text: 'I suppose it would, but I never catch a later train.' 'At what time did you arrive at the station?' 'At ten to eight.
sentences.translation: “我必须在10点钟上班，我的雇主会证明我是按时到了那儿的。

sentences.text: I bought a paper and waited for the train.' 'And you didn't notice anything unusual?' 'I suggest,' said the inspector, 'that you are not telling the truth.
sentences.translation: ”“晚一点儿的车也能送您按时上班吗？

sentences.text: I suggest that you did not catch the 8 o'clock train, but that you caught the 8.25 which would still get you to work on time.
sentences.translation: ”“我认为可以，但我从来不乘晚一点儿的车。

sentences.text: You see, on the morning of the murder, the 8 o'clock train did not run at all.
It broke down at Ferngreen station and was taken off the line.'
sentences.translation: 我买了张报纸，等着车来。

sentences.text: Six men have been trapped in a mine for seventeen hours.
sentences.translation: 6个人被困在矿井里已有17个小时了。

sentences.text: If they are not brought to the surface soon they may lose their lives.
sentences.translation: 如果不把他们尽快救到地面上来，他们就有可能丧生。

sentences.text: However, rescue operations are proving difficult.
sentences.translation: 然而，事实证明营救工作非常困难。

sentences.text: If explosives are used, vibrations will cause the roof of the mine to collapse.
sentences.translation: 如果用炸药爆破，震动会引起矿顶塌落。

sentences.text: Rescue workers are therefore drilling a hole on the north side of the mine.
sentences.translation: 因此，营救人员在矿井的北侧钻了一个洞。

sentences.text: They intend to bring the men up in a special capsule.
sentences.translation: 他们准备用一种特制的容器把这6个人救上来。

sentences.text: If there had not been a hard layer of rock beneath the soil, they would have completed the job in a few hours.
sentences.translation: 如果不是因为土壤下面有一层坚硬的岩石，他们的营救工作仅用几个小时就可以完成了。

sentences.text: As it is, they have been drilling for sixteen hours and they still have a long way to go.
sentences.translation: 实际情况是，他们已连续钻了16个小时了，但离钻透还早着呢。

sentences.text: Meanwhile, a microphone, which was lowered into the mine two hours ago, has enabled the men to keep in touch with their closest relatives.
sentences.translation: 与此同时，两个小时以前放下井去的一只麦克风使井下的人可以与其亲属保持联系。

sentences.text: Though they are running out of food and drink, the men are cheerful and confident that they will get out soon.
sentences.translation: 虽然他们的食物和饮料都快消耗尽了，但这些人的心情很好，坚信他们很快就会出去。

sentences.text: They have been told that rescue operations are progressing smoothly.
sentences.translation: 他们一直被告知营救工作进行得非常顺利。

sentences.text: If they knew how difficult it was to drill through the hard rock, they would lose heart.
sentences.translation: 如果他们知道了钻透那坚硬的岩石有多么困难，他们会丧失信心的。

sentences.text: People will do anything to see a free show -- even if it is a bad one.
sentences.translation: 人们总要想尽办法看不花钱的演出——哪怕是拙劣的演出。

sentences.text: When the news got round that a variety show would be presented at our local cinema by the P. and U.
sentences.translation: 当“皮尤”鸟食公司将在我们当地影院演出喜剧节目的消息传开后， 我们都赶紧跑去观看。

sentences.text: Bird Seed Company, we all rushed to see it.
sentences.translation: 我们不得不排了好几个小时才进得场去。

sentences.text: We had to queue for hours to get in and there must have been several hundred people present just before the show began.
sentences.translation: 在演出开始前场内肯定已有好几百人了。

sentences.text: Unfortunately, the show was one of the dullest we have ever seen.
sentences.translation: 不幸的是，这次演出是我们看过的最乏味的演出了。

sentences.text: Those who failed to get in need not have felt disappointed as many of the artistes who should have appeared did not come.
sentences.translation: 那些没能进到场内的人没有必要感到失望，因为很多应该出场的专业演员都没有来。

sentences.text: The only funny things we heard that evening came from the advertiser at the beginning of the programme.
sentences.translation: 那天晚上唯一有趣的事情是节目开始时那个报幕员的开场白。

sentences.text: He was obviously very nervous and for some minutes stood awkwardly before the microphone.
sentences.translation: 他显然非常紧张，局促不安地在麦克风前站了好几分钟。

sentences.text: As soon as he opened his mouth, everyone burst out laughing.
sentences.translation: 但他刚一开口说话，人们便哄堂大笑起来。

sentences.text: We all know what the poor man should have said, but what he actually said was: 'This is the Poo and Ee Seed Bird Company.
sentences.translation: 我们都明白那个可怜的人应该说些什么，而他实际说的却是：“这是‘浦伊’鸟食公司，好女士们，晚上和先生们！

sentences.text: Good ladies, evening and gentlemen!'
Fish and chips has always been a favourite dish in Britain, but as the oceans have been overfished, fish has become more and more expensive.
sentences.translation: 油煎鱼加炸土豆片一直是英国人喜爱的一道菜，但是随着海洋里的滥捕滥捞，鱼已经变得越来越昂贵。

sentences.text: So it comes as a surprise to learn that giant fish are terrifying the divers on North Sea oil rigs.
sentences.translation: 因此，听说北海石油钻井平台上的潜水员受到巨型鱼类的恐吓，确实很让人吃惊。

sentences.text: Oil rigs have to be repaired frequently and divers, who often have to work in darkness a hundred feet under water, have been frightened out of their wits by giant fish bumping into them as they work.
sentences.translation: 钻井平台需要经常修理，潜水员常常要在水面100英尺以下摸黑工作，他们曾在工作时被撞到他们身上的大鱼吓得惊惶失措。

sentences.text: Now they have had special cages made to protect them from these monsters.
sentences.translation: 现在他们有了特制的笼子，用来保护他们免受大鱼的侵袭。

sentences.text: The fish are not sharks or killer whales, but favourite eating varieties like cod and skate which grow to unnatural sizes, sometimes as much as twelve feet in length.
sentences.translation: 这些鱼并不是鲨鱼或逆戟鲸，而是深受人们喜爱的食用鱼品种，如鳕鱼和鳐鱼，只不过它们长得出奇地大，有时长达12英尺。

sentences.text: Three factors have caused these fish to grow so large: the warm water round the hot oil pipes under the sea; the plentiful supply of food thrown overboard by the crews on the rigs; the total absence of fishing boats around the oil rigs.
sentences.translation: 这些鱼能长得这么大是由3个因素造成的：海底热的输油管道附近的温暖的海水；钻井平台工作人员抛到海里充足的食物；钻井平台周围根本没有捕鱼船只。

sentences.text: As a result, the fish just eat and eat and grow and grow in the lovely warm water.
sentences.translation: 结果是，这些鱼就在可爱的温暖的水流中吃呀吃，长呀长。

sentences.text: Who eats who?
sentences.translation: 究竟谁吃谁呢？

sentences.text: A pilot noticed a balloon which seemed to be making for a Royal Air Force Station nearby.
sentences.translation: 一个飞行员发现了一只气球，它像是正飞往附近的一个皇家空军基地。

sentences.text: He informed the station at once, but no one there was able to explain the mystery.
sentences.translation: 他马上把情况报告了该基地，但那里的人没有一个能解释这到底是怎么回事。

sentences.text: The officer in the control tower was very angry when he heard the news, because balloons can be a great danger to aircraft.
sentences.translation: 控制塔上的官员得知这一消息后，非常气愤，因为气球有可能给飞机造成极大的危险。

sentences.text: He said that someone might be spying on the station and the pilot was ordered to keep track of the strange object.
sentences.translation: 他说可能有人正对基地进行侦察，因此命令那个飞行员跟踪那个奇怪的飞行物。

sentences.text: The pilot managed to circle the balloon for some time.
sentences.translation: 飞行员设法绕着气球飞了一阵。

sentences.text: He could make out three men in a basket under it and one of them was holding field-glasses.
sentences.translation: 他看清了气球下面有3个人呆在一只筐里，其中一个举着望远镜。

sentences.text: When the balloon was over the station, the pilot saw one of the men taking photographs.
sentences.translation: 当气球飞临基地上空时，飞行员看见有一个人在拍照。

sentences.text: Soon afterwards, the balloon began to descend and it landed near an airfield.
sentences.translation: 不久，气球开始降落，在一个停机坪附近着了陆。

sentences.text: The police were called in, but they could not arrest anyone, for the basket contained two Members of Parliament and the Commanding Officer of the station!
sentences.translation: 警察被召来了，但他们却不能逮捕任何人，因为筐里是两名国会议员和一名基地的指挥官！

sentences.text: As the Commanding Officer explained later, one half of the station did not know what the other half was doing!
sentences.translation: 正如指挥官后来解释的那样，基地的这半边不知道那半边正在干什么！

sentences.text: It must have been about two in the morning when I returned home.
sentences.translation: 我回到家时，肯定已是凌晨两点左右了。

sentences.text: I tried to wake up my wife by ringing the doorbell, but she was fast asleep, so I got a ladder from the shed in the garden, put it against the wall, and began climbing towards the bedroom window.
sentences.translation: 我按响了门铃， 试图唤醒我的妻子，但她睡得很熟。

sentences.text: I was almost there when a sarcastic voice below said, 'I don't think the windows need cleaning at this time of the night.' I looked down and nearly fell off the ladder when I saw a policeman.
sentences.translation: 于是，我从花园的小棚里搬来了一个梯子，把它靠在墙边， 开始向卧室的窗口爬去。

sentences.text: I immediately regretted answering in the way I did, but I said, 'I enjoy cleaning windows at night.' 'So do I,' answered the policeman in the same tone.
sentences.translation: 快要爬到窗口时， 下面一个人用讽刺的口吻说：“我看不必在夜里这个时候擦窗子吧。

sentences.text: 'Excuse my interrupting you.
sentences.translation: ” 我向下面看去。

sentences.text: I hate to interrupt a man when he's busy working, but would you mind coming with me to the station?' 'Well, I'd prefer to stay here,' I said.
sentences.translation: 当我看清是一个警察时， 差一点儿从梯子上掉下去。

sentences.text: 'You see, I've forgotten my key.' Fortunately, the shouting woke up my wife who opened the window just as the policeman had started to climb towards me.
sentences.translation: 我回答了他的话， 但马上又后悔不该那样说，我是这样说的：“我喜欢在夜里擦窗子。

sentences.text: One of the most famous monuments in the world, the Statue of Liberty, was presented to the United States of America in the nineteenth century by the people of France.
sentences.translation: 世界上最著名的纪念碑之一的自由女神雕像是在19世纪时由法国人民赠送给美国的。

sentences.text: The great statue, which was designed by the sculptor Auguste Bartholdi, took ten years to complete.
sentences.translation: 这座由雕像家奥古斯特·巴索尔地设计的巨大雕像是用10年时间雕像刻成的。

sentences.text: The actual figure was made of copper supported by a metal framework which had been especially constructed by Eiffel.
sentences.translation: 这座雕像的主体是用铜制成的，由艾菲尔特制的金属框架支撑着。

sentences.text: Before it could be transported to the United States, a site had to be found for it and a pedestal had to be built.
sentences.translation: 在雕像被运往美国之前，必须为它选好一块场地，同时必须建造一个基座。

sentences.text: The site chosen was an island at the entrance of New York Harbour.
sentences.translation: 场地选在了纽约港入口处的一个鸟上。

sentences.text: By 1884, a statue which was 151 feet tall had been erected in Paris.
sentences.translation: 到1884年，一座高度达151英尺的雕像在巴黎竖立起来了。

sentences.text: The following year, it was taken to pieces and sent to America.
sentences.translation: 第二年，它被拆成若干小块，运到美国。

sentences.text: By the end of October 1886, the statue had been put together again and it was officially presented to the American people by Bartholdi.
sentences.translation: 到1886年10月底，这座雕像被重新组装起来，由巴索尔地正式赠送给美国人民。

sentences.text: Ever since then, the great monument has been a symbol of liberty for the millions of people who have passed through New York Harbour to make their homes in America.
sentences.translation: 从那时起，这座伟大的纪念碑对通过纽约港进入美国定居的千百万人来说就一直是自由的象征。

sentences.text: Experiments have proved that children can be instructed in swimming at a very early age.
sentences.translation: 实验证明，儿童在很小的时候就可以开始学习游泳。

sentences.text: At a special swimming pool in Los Angeles, children become expert at holding their breath under water even before they can walk.
sentences.translation: 在洛杉矶的一个特设的游泳池里，孩子们甚至在还没有学会走路时就已经能熟练地在水下屏住呼吸了。

sentences.text: Babies of two months old do not appear to be reluctant to enter the water.
sentences.translation: 两个月的婴儿并未显得不愿意入水。

sentences.text: It is not long before they are so accustomed to swimming that they can pick up weights from the floor of the pool.
sentences.translation: 他们很快便适应了游泳，以致能捡起池底的物品。

sentences.text: A game that is very popular with these young swimmers is the underwater tricycle race.
sentences.translation: 这些幼小的游泳运动员非常喜爱的一种游戏是水下三轮车比赛。

sentences.text: Tricycles are lined up on the floor of the pool seven feet under water.
sentences.translation: 三轮车并排放在7英尺深的游泳池底上。

sentences.text: The children compete against each other to reach the other end of the pool.
sentences.translation: 孩子们比赛看谁先到达游泳池的另一端。

sentences.text: Many pedal their tricycles, but most of them prefer to push or drag them.
sentences.translation: 很多孩子用脚蹬车，但多数孩子更愿意推或是拉着三轮车。

sentences.text: Some children can cover the whole length of the pool without coming up for breath even once.
sentences.translation: 有些孩子能够跑完游泳池的全长而不用露出水面换气。

sentences.text: Whether they will ever become future Olympic champions, only time will tell.
sentences.translation: 他们将来是否能成为奥林匹克的冠军，这只能由时间来作出回答。

sentences.text: Meanwhile, they should encourage those among us who cannot swim five yards before they are gasping for air.
sentences.translation: 与此同时，他们对我们中的那些游不了5码就已喘不过气来的人应该是种鼓舞。

sentences.text: When the Ambassador of Escalopia returned home for lunch, his wife got a shock.
sentences.translation: 当艾斯卡罗比亚国的大使回到家吃午饭时，把他的夫人吓了一跳。

sentences.text: He looked pale and his clothes were in a frightful state.
sentences.translation: 他面色苍白，衣服也搞得不成样子。

sentences.text: 'What has happened?' she asked.
sentences.translation: “发生了什么事？

sentences.text: 'How did your clothes get into such a mess?' 'A fire extinguisher, my dear,' answered the Ambassador drily.
sentences.translation: ”她问，“你的衣服怎么搞得一塌糊涂？

sentences.text: 'University students set the Embassy on fire this morning.' 'Good heavens!' exclaimed his wife.
sentences.translation: ”“灭火器弄的，亲爱的，”大使冷冷地回答，“今天上午大学生们放火点着了大使馆。

sentences.text: 'And where were you at the time?' 'I was in my office as usual,' answered the Ambassador.
sentences.translation: ”“天啊！

sentences.text: 'The fire broke out in the basement.

sentences.text: I went down immediately, of course, and that fool, Horst, aimed a fire extinguisher at me.
sentences.translation: ”“我和往常一样，在办公室里，”大使回答说。

sentences.text: He thought I was on fire.
sentences.translation: “地下室突然着火，我当然马上下去了。

sentences.text: I must definitely get that fellow posted.' The Ambassador's wife went on asking questions, when she suddenly noticed a big hole in her husband's hat.
sentences.translation: 但那个傻瓜霍斯特把灭火器对准了我。

sentences.text: A Festival for the Dead is held once a year in Japan.
sentences.translation: 日本每年过一次“亡灵节”。

sentences.text: This festival is a cheerful occasion, for on this day, the dead are said to return to their homes and they are welcomed by the living.
sentences.translation: 这个节日是个欢乐的日子，因为在这一天，据说死去的人要回到他们的家里来，活着的人则对他们表示欢迎。

sentences.text: As they are expected to be hungry after their long journey, food is laid out for them.
sentences.translation: 因为预料到他们在经过长途旅行之后会感到饥饿，所以为他们摆放好了食品。

sentences.text: Specially-made lanterns are hung outside each house to help the dead to find their way.
sentences.translation: 特制的灯笼挂在各家的门外，为的是帮助亡灵看清道路。

sentences.text: All night long, people dance and sing.
sentences.translation: 整个夜晚人们载歌载舞。

sentences.text: In the early morning, the food that had been laid out for the dead is thrown into a river or into the sea as it is considered unlucky for anyone living to eat it.
sentences.translation: 一大早，人们便把为死者摆放的食品扔进河中或海里，因为人们认为活着的人吃了这些东西是不吉利的。

sentences.text: In towns that are near the sea, the tiny lanterns which had been hung in the streets the night before, are placed into the water when the festival is over.
sentences.translation: 在靠海的城镇中，头天夜里挂在大街小巷的小灯笼在节后就放在了水里。

sentences.text: Thousands of lanterns slowly drift out to sea guiding the dead on their return journey to the other world.
sentences.translation: 成千上万只灯笼慢慢漂向大海，指引着亡灵返回另一个世界。

sentences.text: This is a moving spectacle, for crowds of people stand on the shore watching the lanterns drifting away until they can be seen no more.
sentences.translation: 这是一个感人的场面，人们成群地伫立在海岸上，注视着灯笼远去，直到再也看不见为止。
